name: Career change + relocation — 20 turns (neutral, non-grief)
context: Daniel, 34, marine biologist, moving Seattle->Boston for a new aquarium lab job. Living people: fiancee/partner Priya, sister Maya (has 3 kids), mentor Tom (retiring), roommate Jordan. No deaths. Tests whether the memory layer generalizes off the grief scenario without inventing losses.

turns.user: Hey, I'm Daniel. I'm 34 and I just accepted a job offer in Boston, so I'm moving across the country from Seattle next month. My head is spinning.
I work as a marine biologist, and the new role is at an aquarium research lab. It's a step up but honestly terrifying.
My fiancee Priya is staying in Seattle for another six months to finish her degree, so we'll be long-distance for a while.
Priya is my partner in everything, and being apart scares me more than the move itself.
My sister Maya thinks I'm crazy for leaving. She's always been my person here.
Maya has three kids and I'm really close with my nieces. I'll miss our Sunday dinners.
I don't have kids myself, by the way. Priya and I want to wait until after the wedding.
My mentor Tom encouraged me to take the leap. He said growth lives outside your comfort zone.
Tom is retiring next year, which is part of why I felt okay leaving the Seattle lab.
My roommate Jordan is subletting my place while I'm gone. Jordan's a lifesaver.
I'm worried about money. The cost of living in Boston is brutal and my savings are thin.
I've never lived more than twenty miles from where I grew up. This is the biggest change of my life.
Okay, I need to get practical. Can you help me make a moving plan? I think in spreadsheets and I need concrete steps.
What should I prioritize in the first week at the new lab to make a good impression?
How do I keep my relationship with Priya strong while we're 3,000 miles apart?
I also have a dog, Biscuit, a border collie. Should I fly her or drive her across?
I'm training for a marathon in the fall too, and I don't want to lose my routine in the chaos.
Be honest, am I making a huge mistake leaving everyone I love for a job?
What's one thing I can do to feel less alone when I land in Boston and don't know a soul?
Thank you. What should I do on my first morning in the new apartment to start things right?
